steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The phrase before the blank, 'lost works like Lucan's poem praising Emperor Nero, *Laudes Neronis*—no copy of which exists—', contains the plural noun 'works' as its subject.

steps.title: Apply subject‑verb agreement
steps.content:
A plural subject requires a plural verb; 'have become' is the plural present‑perfect form that agrees with 'lost works'.
The other three choices use singular verbs ('is becoming', 'has become', 'becomes') which do not match the plural subject.
That matches Choice A — have become.
